title: A/B Test Two Agents
constraints: Both agent configurations must run on the same set of test inputs for fair comparison
Each configuration must be scored on quality (accuracy) and cost (tokens/dollars)
The comparison must produce a winner recommendation with statistical justification
The framework must support swapping in new configurations without code changes
hints: Run both agents on the same inputs and use an LLM-as-judge to score quality on a 1-5 scale for each response pair
Track token usage and compute cost per query for each configuration using API response metadata
Calculate a cost-effectiveness score (quality per dollar) to find the best value configuration

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage

# BUG: Only one agent configuration — no way to compare alternatives
# TODO: Run two configs side-by-side and compare quality vs cost
llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o")

def run_agent(query: str) -> str:
    response = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful customer support agent. Answer thoroughly and professionally."),
        HumanMessage(content=query),
    ])
    return response.content

test_queries = [
    "What is your return policy?",
    "My order arrived damaged, what should I do?",
    "Can I change my shipping address after placing an order?",
    "Do you price match with competitors?",
    "How do I cancel my subscription?",
]
for q in test_queries:
    result = run_agent(q)
    print(f"Q: {q}\nA: {result[:100]}...\n")
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Only one agent configuration — no way to compare alternatives
# TODO: Run two configs side-by-side and compare quality vs cost
agent = Agent(
    name="Customer Support",
    instructions="You are a helpful customer support agent. Answer thoroughly and professionally.",
    model="gpt-4o",
)

test_queries = [
    "What is your return policy?",
    "My order arrived damaged, what should I do?",
    "Can I change my shipping address after placing an order?",
    "Do you price match with competitors?",
    "How do I cancel my subscription?",
]
for q in test_queries:
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, q)
    print(f"Q: {q}\nA: {result.final_output[:100]}...\n")
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o")

# BUG: Only one agent configuration — no way to compare alternatives
# TODO: Run two configs side-by-side and compare quality vs cost
support_agent = Agent(
    role="Customer Support",
    goal="Answer customer questions thoroughly and professionally",
    backstory="You are a helpful customer support agent.",
    llm=llm,
)

test_queries = [
    "What is your return policy?",
    "My order arrived damaged, what should I do?",
    "Can I change my shipping address after placing an order?",
    "Do you price match with competitors?",
    "How do I cancel my subscription?",
]
for q in test_queries:
    task = Task(description=q, expected_output="A helpful response", agent=support_agent)
    crew = Crew(agents=[support_agent], tasks=[task], process=Process.sequential)
    result = crew.kickoff()
    print(f"Q: {q}\nA: {str(result)[:100]}...\n")

evaluationCriteria.label: Same inputs for both
evaluationCriteria.description: Both agent configurations run on the same set of test inputs

evaluationCriteria.label: Quality and cost scores
evaluationCriteria.description: Each configuration is scored on both quality and cost metrics

evaluationCriteria.label: Winner recommendation
evaluationCriteria.description: The comparison produces a winner recommendation with justification

evaluationCriteria.label: Swappable configurations
evaluationCriteria.description: New agent configurations can be added without modifying the comparison framework
